behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role."
Slot B: Failure boundary is orchestrator-to-reviewer module handoff (role->instruction mapping plus shared document fan-out).
Slot C: A Unit test of only the payload builder misses cross-module wiring bugs where packs are swapped during parallel dispatch.
Decision: Integration, based on Slot B handoff boundary and Slot C lower-level miss-proof. Integration-brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess required; no model-output quality assertion; no multi-agentic sequence under test. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text is about "sends ... instruction pack" correctness, not reviewer judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher + invocation adapter + role config in-process with spy/stub reviewer endpoints; dispatch one contract and assert all three calls received byte-identical source document, and each role received its intended instruction-pack identifier/content.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "The live clause extractor identifies key terms ... present in the contract."
Slot B: Correctness boundary is real model extraction quality on legal text.
Slot C: Integration with stubbed extractor always returns canned terms and cannot reveal real misses on obligations/penalties phrasing.
Decision: Agentic, from Slot B model-judgment dependency and Slot C miss-proof against stub masking. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because text targets one reviewer capability (single agentic decision), not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a labeled legal-contract set; assert per-category recall/precision (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) meet acceptance thresholds and that required key terms are present in extracted output.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation."
Slot B: Failure boundary is config serialization/propagation between policy module and invocation module.
Slot C: Unit-testing only serializer logic misses wiring defects where caller omits or overrides policy at call site.
Decision: Integration, using Slot B cross-module propagation and Slot C lower-level blind spot. Integration-brake check: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no dependence on model output content; no chained agentic steps. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text is only "configuration ... passed correctly," not whether risk judgments are correct.
behaviors.test_description: Execute service-layer invocation path with a stub risk-assessor client that records requests; provide non-default policy and assert outbound payload includes exact policy fields/versions and no unintended defaults.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "live risk assessor flags language ... including indirect or euphemistic phrasing."
Slot B: Correctness boundary is model semantic judgment under policy, especially non-literal phrasing.
Slot C: Stubbed/integration tests with canned labels cannot expose failures on euphemistic language interpretation.
Decision: Agentic, driven by Slot B semantic model dependence and Slot C evidence that mocks hide real failures. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior concerns one reviewer’s decision quality, not multi-step agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real risk-assessor model on a curated corpus containing explicit and euphemistic risky clauses with gold labels; verify detection quality (e.g., recall on euphemisms plus precision floor) meets policy thresholds.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation."
Slot B: Failure boundary is orchestrator->compliance-invocation state handoff.
Slot C: Unit-testing profile builder alone misses call-site mapping errors (wrong jurisdiction/profile attached to request).
Decision: Integration, based on Slot B handoff boundary and Slot C adjacent-lower miss-proof. Integration-brake check: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess; no model-output quality assertion; no multi-agentic chain. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because text specifies parameter passing correctness, not compliance decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run compliance invocation pipeline in-process with a recording stub client; supply known jurisdiction/profile inputs and assert exact propagation in outbound request fields, including enum/value normalization.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements."
Slot B: Correctness boundary is real model/regulatory reasoning output against contract text.
Slot C: Integration with deterministic fake outcomes cannot detect real false pass/fail decisions.
Decision: Agentic, from Slot B real-model judgment requirement and Slot C proof that lower-level stubs mask failures. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because this behavior targets a single reviewer’s determination, not sequential multi-agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real compliance-checker calls on labeled contracts spanning compliant/non-compliant cases per configured regulations; assert classification accuracy/recall on violations and acceptable false-pass ceiling.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution."
Slot B: Failure boundary is deterministic merge-rule logic on provided reviewer outputs.
Slot C: Raising to Integration is unnecessary because direct function inputs already reproduce the conflict-preservation bug without module/I/O dependencies.
Decision: Unit, justified by Slot B local merge-function boundary and Slot C showing higher level adds no unique failure exposure. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text isolates "merge step" logic and does not require dispatch/network/model calls.
behaviors.test_description: Call merge function/object directly with synthetic extractor and risk-assessor outputs referencing the same clause with conflicting labels; assert output retains both assessments and reviewer attribution metadata for that clause.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report ... on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings."
Slot B: Failure boundary spans multiple agentic outputs plus orchestration/aggregation across stages.
Slot C: Agentic-only single-step tests miss cross-step loss/overwrite bugs when combining three independent reviewer results.
Decision: Workflow, based on Slot B multi-step agentic pipeline and Slot C lower-level miss-proof for handoff/drop failures. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly requires sequenced orchestration across multiple reviewer steps and merge.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline against a real contract using live extractor/risk/compliance reviewers in parallel, then merge; verify unified report contains the complete union of reviewer findings (with stable IDs/attribution) and no dropped findings across collection and merge stages.
